entities.name: Noah
entities.raw_quote: the preservation of this knowledge thus attributed to Noah is characteristic of the methods of Paganism...

entities.name: Seth
entities.raw_quote: it may be remarked that the name Seth is synonymous with Shem...

entities.name: Cush
entities.raw_quote: it is very probable that Cush... may... have received instructions from the spirits he invoked as gods...

entities.name: Nephilim

attributes.role: interacted with humans pre- and postdiluvianly

entities.raw_quote: The term 'Sons of God'... is only used in the Old Testament to express the angels... The original word translated 'giants' is not 'giants' but 'Hephilim,' meaning 'Fallen Ones'

entities.name: Chrysor
entities.raw_quote: Chrysor, who was worshipped after his death, was seemingly the first of the Pagan gods

entities.name: Oannes
entities.raw_quote: Berosus speaks of Oannes and the Annedoti as 'daemons'... Oannes was actually worshipped in after ages as one of the chief gods of Paganism under his own name, Oannes, or as Dagon

entities.name: Annedoti
entities.raw_quote: Berosus speaks of Oannes and the Annedoti as 'daemons'...

entities.name: Satan
entities.raw_quote: Satan is said to be him who 'deceiveth the whole world'... Ha Nāhash—'the Serpent'

entities.name: Ghemschid / Djemschid

attributes.role: king who married daughter of a demon

entities.raw_quote: The Persian account says that the father of Ghemschid founded the cities of Babylon and Nineveh... This is clearly Babylon, the beginning of the kingdom of Nimrod

entities.name: Belus / Bel
entities.raw_quote: Bel, who was the first king in Babylon many years after that event, is represented as arranging the circumstances of the Flood

entities.name: Osiris
entities.raw_quote: Osiris, the god of the Egyptians... their true relations to their human originals is sometimes misplaced

entities.name: Jupiter
entities.raw_quote: the Jupiter of the Greeks and Romans... are by these writers entirely removed from the events of human history

entities.name: Phlegyae
entities.raw_quote: The impious Phlegyae were said by the Greeks to be descended from Mars and Chrysa

entities.name: Hermes / Hephsestus
entities.raw_quote: the tradition of the first Hermes, or Hephsestus... implies that the worship of the gods... first originated in the antediluvian world

summary: This chunk explores the antediluvian and postdiluvian origins of spirit communication, linking biblical Nephilim (Fallen Ones) to pagan demon worship. It argues that Cush/Nimrod revived pre-Flood magical traditions involving spirit invocation, and identifies Oannes/Dagon and Chrysor as Nephilim or demonic beings who taught humans necromantic arts. The text connects Noahic tradition with later mythologies, suggesting pagan deities were deified postdiluvian spirits or demon-human hybrids.
key_passages: it is very probable that Cush, the originator of postdiluvian idolatry, may... have received instructions from the spirits he invoked as gods, as to the means of communicating with them and enlisting their powers on his behalf; or, in other words, the principles of magic and sorcery
The original word translated 'giants' is not 'giants' but 'Hephilim,' meaning 'Fallen Ones'
the intercourse of these 'fallen ones' with the human race in the past... was the general belief of the Jews and the early Christian Church
Berosus speaks of Oannes and the Annedoti as 'daemons' which is but another name for the Pagan gods
Satan is said to be him who 'deceiveth the whole world' (Rev. xii. 9), and the chief of these Annedoti... may well have been Satan himself
